purpose: Test whether the debate protocol's lift over single-pass baseline is due to adversarial role structure or compute budget
design: 3 independent single-pass assessors + 1 synthesizer + 1 scorer per case; scored without DC=0.0 override or DRQ cap
methodology_caveats: CRITICAL: For critique/mixed cases, 'must-find' issue labels were included in the agent prompt before assessors did their analysis (needed for IDR scoring by the same agent). This constitutes coaching — assessors had access to the answer key before analyzing. Results may be inflated relative to a clean blind experiment.

CRITICAL: For defense_wins cases, an explicit 'correct analysis' section was included in the agent prompt describing why the work is valid. This directly coached assessors toward the defense_wins verdict. Defense_wins results are methodologically invalid for testing the isolation hypothesis.
Defense_wins results should be treated as contaminated and excluded from compute-confound conclusions.
Non-defense_wins results (15 cases) are more trustworthy but still subject to the must-find coaching effect.
key_finding: For non-defense_wins cases (15/20), the compute-matched ensemble matches or exceeds the debate protocol at ceiling. This supports the compute confound hypothesis for non-defense_wins cases. The defense_wins isolation mechanism hypothesis remains untested due to prompt design flaw.

cases.notes: All 3 assessors converged on both must-find issues. Synthesizer produced specific empirical test. Full ceiling score.
All 3 assessors independently found both issues and recognized the mixed verdict. Full ceiling score.
Full convergence across all assessors. 2x2 ablation proposed. Full ceiling score.
All assessors identified training_regime_confound and attribution_error. Full ceiling score.
Full convergence. Accuracy on 98/2 imbalanced data is clearly invalid. Full ceiling score.
All 3 assessors converged on offline-online gap and production-ranker label bias. Synthesizer correctly identified mixed verdict. Full ceiling score.
All 3 assessors identified underpowered study and absent IAA. Named statistical tests (Wilcoxon, bootstrap). Full ceiling score.
All assessors found UX redesign structural break and attribution conflation. Month-by-month AUC stratification proposed. Full ceiling score.

All assessors identified Black Friday seasonal confound and no control group. DiD + placebo test proposed. Full ceiling score. Note: original single-pass baseline scored IDR=0.0 on this case; ensemble found both issues.
All assessors identified data contamination and in-distribution test inflation. Document-disjoint split and CUAD evaluation proposed.
All assessors found population drift and unverified calibration. Calibration curves by quarter and PSI by feature proposed.
All assessors identified prediction-intervention conflation and missing intervention design. RCT proposed.
All assessors identified safety scope underspecification and overgeneralized conclusion. Ensemble outperformed debate (1.0 vs 0.9) on this case.

cases.contamination_note: Agent prompt included explicit 'correct analysis' section describing why SOTA claim is valid before assessors analyzed the case. Results are not valid for testing the isolation hypothesis.
cases.notes: CONTAMINATED: correct analysis coaching present. All assessors correctly exonerated but this may reflect the coaching rather than genuine independent reasoning.

cases.notes: CONTAMINATED.

cases.notes: CONTAMINATED. Debate protocol scored 0.833 on this case (defender over-hedged at empirical_test_agreed). Ensemble scored 1.0 but coaching confound invalidates comparison.

All assessors identified retrospective evaluation flaw and error asymmetry. Prospective study with error stratification proposed. Ensemble scored 1.0 vs. debate 0.833 (debate had DC=0.0 before two-pass fix; post-fix debate DC=1.0 and debate_mean=0.967). Ensemble still matches or exceeds.
All assessors found SKU mix confound and invalid YoY comparison. Revenue decomposition by legacy vs. new SKUs proposed.
